text: This way.
Come on, hurry up.
Better to stick together down here.
Whew. That was close.
Sneaking is overrated, eh?
Hmm. That doesn't go anywhere.
No going back that way, now.
That thing was a dragon. No doubt. Just like the children's stories and the legends. The harbingers of the End Times.
Almost there.
I'd rather not tangle with her right now. Let's try to sneak by. Just take it nice and slow, and watch where you step.
That looks like the way out! I knew we'd make it!
The ivory from this tusk will be perfect for the inlay on my amulets.
When Elgrim said we were setting up shop down here, I told him he would catch his death.
The dirt, the moisture... it's disgusting. Who wants to spend their life living like a skeever?
But that old bellyacher just fed me some nonsense about light ruining his mixtures and being close to the water... agh.
It's about time; I sent them a letter weeks ago. That Elgrim... he'd forget his own pants if his wife didn't help him dress.
Here you go. Tell Hafjorg that she can chip off whatever she thinks is a fair trade for their time.
Did you ever have to fight off bandits, Frida? Like in your stories?
Leigelf is a good man, and I'm not running out on him now.
You mean Hildolf the Skull-Splitter? Remember when we rounded up the townspeople and dragged him out of that cave?
Why, I once fought alongside your father when we were both younger. Saved him from getting his fool head sliced off.
Oh, got a bit of interest in your boss do you?
Those were the days. I'd never seen my husband fight so bravely before.
Who were you fighting against?
Aye, to your husband. Divines bless his memory.
No, not that it's any of your business.
Oh, he was young, and handsome. I had half a mind to go after him, myself, before I met my husband.
Oh, dearie, it was so long ago. But I do remember the celebration we had afterwards. Mead never tasted so good.
Did I ever tell you about the day when Skald the Elder was Skald the Younger?
Thoring, do you remember the time when that wolf pack came clamoring into town?
Tough day at the mines. I swear the ore is getting harder to dig.
I can't imagine Skald ever being young.
Frida, you aren't saying...
How could I forget? That old alpha male almost took off my leg.
You're just getting older.
Never did find out why they came sprawling into town like that.
I was a beauty in my day, dearie. Don't act so surprised.
That's why I come here to talk to you. You're all heart.
Animals in The Pale don't need a reason to attack Dawnstar. It's just their nature.
Dawnstar has always held its own against the bandits and wild animals of The Pale.
You should quit working in the eastern mine. Leigelf's no good to work for.
Now, who was it that kidnapped the jarl's son 20 years ago?
I swear, if I wasn't around, Elgrim would forget to eat his meals. How we make any coin at all is beyond me.
In fact, I need to arrange for someone to pick up an ore sample for me in Shor's Stone... goodness knows Elgrim won't lift a finger to do it.
Thank you, marsh-friend.
Thank you, land-strider.
Be careful, the road to Shor's Stone could be dangerous.
Don't forget us if you need supplies for your journey.
Auxiliary.
Quaestor.
Praefect.
Tribune.
Legate.
It's always good to see someone I can count on.
The general expects so much with so little.
The sooner we end this rebellion, the sooner I'll be able to sleep at night.
Ulfric... what have you done my old friend?
Stormcloaks... even the name of their rebellion stinks of Ulfric's vanity. He wants to be king. That's all this is about. One man's ambition.
I don't fight for the glory of the Empire, nor do I glory in the death of my kinsmen. I fight to bring peace and order to my homeland.
Damnit! Why can't Ulfric see he's tearing Skyrim to pieces?
Sometimes I wonder if the gods have abandoned us... as we have abandoned them.
What I do, I do for Skyrim and her people.
When we destroy the rebellion, perhaps the Thalmor will call off their damned inquisitions. It's just making things worse.
Sometimes I miss being out there in the middle of the action.
Unblooded.
Ice-Veins.
Bone-Breaker.
Snow-Hammer.
Stormblade.
Always good to see someone I can trust.
Sometimes I wonder if Ulfric understands what I'm dealing with out here.
That godless Emperor can shove his damn treaty into the steaming nether-reaches of my you-know-where.
My hatred for the Empire is exceeded only by my hatred for their witch-elf puppet-masters.
What will Skyrim have become if her children submitted to tyranny?
The Empire would have us become their slaves as they have become slaves to the Thalmor. I will not live as a slave, nor will I die as one.
The Empire is weak. That is why we fight them. Skyrim must be ruled by the strong.
Some say Ulfric desires to be king and nothing more... have they not seen that his anger floats on a sea of tears?
Countless dead haunt countless sleepless nights... when Skyrim becomes whole again, perhaps so will I.
When we have reclaimed our homeland from the Imperials, we will take the fight to the Thalmor. They are the true enemy. Make no mistake.
If there was someone else Ulfric could trust with all this planning nonsense, I would be out there breaking heads with the rest of you.
Buy it? This? An Elder Council amulet?
Oh yes. Oh yes, indeed.
Uh, wait just one moment...
Here... is a letter of credit. Usable, by Astrid only, for any service or item I can provide. As per our standard arrangement.
You bring that back to your lovely mistress. With my regards.
The Elder Council...
Oh, now that explains quite a bit. Motierre, you naughty, naughty boy. Hiring the Dark Brotherhood to help you rise beyond your station. Delicious.
Was Mallory willing to buy the amulet?
Let's see...
Where oh where did you get this? No, don't answer - I don't want to know.
This is an amulet of the Emperor's Elder Council. Specially crafted for each member. Worth a small fortune. Ain't somethin' you'd give up lightly.
Look, it ain't my business to tell the Dark Brotherhood its business, but if you killed a member of the Elder Council, you'd better belie-
Good, you're back. Well, what did Mallory have to say? Is the amulet authentic?
Oh. Oh, I see.
Well, you're makin' friends all over, ain't ya?
So, uh... how is Astrid doin' these days? Tell her to stop by some time. We can have a drink. Catch up.
Ah, but business! Of course. What kind of business?
Oh. Oh, I see.
Well now, how is Astrid doin' these days, uh? Tell her to stop by some time. We can have a drink. Catch up.
Ah, but we can discuss that later, yeah? What does the Brotherhood need?
Somethin' ya need? Anything for the guild.
Ah, now you must be lost. Best ya scurry off while you're able. The Ratway, well, it has a habit of swallowin' up the uninvited.
Poor Cicero. Dear Cicero. Such a humble servant. But he will never hear my voice. For he is not the Listener.
I knew I could count on you.
Good. Don't die out there.
We all have faith in you.
Go with the Nine.
Talos keep you.
Don't screw this up.
Good. And remember - keep the gods, and they'll keep you.
Excellent. Put the fear of the Nine into those motherless dogs.
That's what I wanted to hear. Good luck out there.
Good luck out there.
We do this for the good of Skyrim and her people. Never forget that.
Make me proud.
Rexus. The items.
Have a client lookin' to have a rival's business ledger changed. Don't know the reason, and don't want to know. Count you in?
Got a personal request from Brynjolf on this numbers job. He says we may hit 'em hard later, and he wants to prep their books. Help us out?
These marks keep their ledgers all neat and tidy, but they're ripe for some change, if you know what I mean. Take it?
All right. Got a simple one. Just get in, change the books and be back in time for last call. So how's about it?
With Vici's murder, you've started us down a path the Dark Brotherhood hasn't traveled in centuries. The assassination of an emperor.
And now, your reward. A unique spell to summon a legend of the Dark Brotherhood. His soul serves us now in death, as his body once did in life.
Unfortunately, in your zeal, you failed to eliminate Vici while she addressed her guests, as directed. I'm sorry, but your bonus is forfeit.
Now, then. Time to proceed to the next stage of the plan. Go and speak with Gabriella. She's been helping me arrange your next contract.
Make sure to get yourself something to drink.
You hungry? There's plenty of food. Eat up, now.
What's going on? Why would somebody do that?
When my aunt got married, they had a cake that was three feet tall. I got really sick that day.
I heard Vittoria Vici is the Emperor's cousin. I wish he came to the wedding. I never saw a real live emperor before.
By the gods. The blood! The blood!
I love weddings, don't you?
I just can't wait to be a bride someday. Someday...
I didn't do it! You saw! I didn't do it!
I've eaten more in the last two hours than I have in the last two weeks.
If I'm really lucky, I'll get to kiss the bride for good luck.
By all the gods, the groom has been killed. At his own wedding! I can only think of my own husband...
A marriage of both love and political advantage. A rare thing indeed.
Oh, no no no... This is so wrong...
I'm so glad Vittoria and Asgeir chose a public reception. That way the entire city can share in their holy bond.
Oh, did you miss the ceremony? It was rather quick, actually. The couple seemed more concerned with their reception.
I just came to sing! This is a horror show!
Ahem. Mi mi mi mi. Pardon me. Just, um, warming up. Never know when I may be called upon for a ballad.
The thought of paying a dowry to that - that... Stormcloak turns my stomach.
My son is dead! At his own wedding! Only those Imperial dogs would have dared do something like this! Damn them all to Oblivion!
They say this marriage is the first step towards peace. Who in Shor's name wants peace? Pfft.
My boy, he always did have an eye for the pretty ones. That Vittoria ain't bad, for an Imperial.
By Ysmir, this is horrible! I never wanted the girl dead, I swear!
To think, my little boy is married. And to an Imperial. Oh, they grow up so fast.
If my Asgeir is happy, then I'm happy. Really.
There are all sorts of people wandering around carryin' their valuables on their person... just waitin' for someone like you to pick their pocket.
So, you in for a little fishin'?
Sure, I've got a mark for you. Stole something quite important from a friend of the Guild and we take care of our own. You in?
One of our marks seems to have an item of value we want to add to our vault. Get it for us and you'll receive the usual pay. Sound good?
Got ourselves a freelancer thinks he can muscle in on our territory. How about you teach him some manners, eh?
Maven wants this mark's most prized possession. The bloody fool carries it on his person all the time. So, think you can handle it?
Brynjolf wants this mark's head on a pike, but we'll settle for what the bastard's carryin' instead. Can I count on you?
Got a mark taxed with carryin' a heavy item. Care to lighten' the load?
This brilliant soul decided to pick a fight with a fellow Guild mate. Time for them to pay the piper. Will you help out?
I've got a mark who just got an item from a relative outside of Skyrim. They probably should have been quieter about it. You in?
We'd better stick together down here.
There may be a way out, down this way.
I guess that'll teach me to try to reason with a Stormcloak.
Let's see where this goes. We can't be the only survivors.
Find yourself a weapon. You're going to need it.
We'd better get out of here. I don't think this place is going to stand up to much more of this.
Don't worry. We'll get out of here, somehow.
Gods above! Look at that!
I... it's true, isn't it? You really are Dragonborn.
What an interesting idea. I think if someone were to do something like that, there would definitely be a certain amount of loot to be shared...
Yes, if someone were to put out the lighthouse fire and then meet me on the docks afterwards... I could certainly direct them to that loot.
My time has come. I am old. Too old to become chief. It would be wrong for me to take wives at this age. So I will die.
Malacath has given me a vision of a glorious death. I am to wait here until it finds me.
As you can see, it has not yet arrived.
Great! Thanks! Bastard stole from me. I'll catch him though!
Yes! How did you get this? Never mind. Here, it's not much, but it's something.
I'm going to track down that thief and murder him!
Hmm. We shall see.
You should leave. I don't want you scaring off my good death.
Perhaps...
Are you sure about this?
Get out of here!
This is my treasure!
I'm not kidding. I'll hurt you!
No time to talk. Snitch or double-cross me and I'll kill you. I mean it!
Well, if you do, grab him and yell. Bastard stole from me.
Indeed. One should find his death while he can still call himself a proper man.
We Orc men are not like these Nords and Imperials who carry on until they are grey and feeble and their hair falls out.
To cling to something past its usefulness is unseemly, how much more so when that thing is you?
I am waiting for a good death.
If you are not here to grant me a good death, then you can leave.
Take this and hold onto it. I'll be back for it later. I'll pay you good. Don't lose it.
Did you see someone run past just now?
Yes. Were I to simply lay down and die, it would not please Malacath.
You're passing through Solitude? Maybe you're looking to make some easy gold, yes?
What?! This is exactly why... don't they understand it's hard enough to fight a war without these juvenile attempts to undermine authority?
You're leaving? Okay, we'll just work on the verse in a bit.
I'll tell everyone we're ready, but we'll start the festival at dusk.
Come talk to me after dark. We'll get the festival started when you do.
My coat? What do you need my coat for?
If you say so. If this were anyone but you I would lock them up.
Take it. Take the coat, have your fun. I'll see you at the festival.
Well then, what are you waiting for? Get in there and earn your pay.
Any progress on finding the mask?
You there! As an officer of the Imperial Legion, I require your assistance to complete my mission.
And what, you couldn't read it yourself? Fine, give it here a moment.
Everyone in town knows about the Black Door, and to never go near it.
Did you hear? People have been seen coming in and out of the Black Door. Divines have mercy.
Yyyyyyyaaaaaahhhhhhh!
Wwwwwwrrrraaaaaahhhhh!
Hhhhhhhaaaaaa!
Hhhhhhuuuuuuaaaaaa!
Yyyyrrrraaaaahhhhh!
Rrrraaaaaaggghhhh!
Aaaaaahhhhaaaaa!
Hhhhhuuuuuzzzzzaaaaahhhhh!
Rrrrraaaaa!
Yyyyyeeeaaaaahhhhhh!
Oh my. That...
You... you go first.
What've you been staring at your feet this whole time? You see that big city up ahead of you? Markarth?
Head over there, unless you feel like helping my wife pick crops. And then 50 years later you'll wake up and still be here.
What kind of question is that? Look at me! Is this a young man's face?
I've been here 50 years, and nothing is going to move me off my land.
As part of your initiation, Master Einarth will allow you to tap into his understanding of "Ro."
Well, that is how the rest of us learn Shouts. As Dragonborn, you can absorb a slain dragon's life force and knowledge directly.
But learning a Word of Power is only the first step... you must unlock its meaning through constant practice in order to use it in a Shout.
All Shouts are made up of three Words of Power. As you master each Word, your Shout will become progressively stronger.
Ro means "Balance" in the dragon tongue. Combine it with Fus - "Force" - to focus your Thu'um more sharply.
Do you think the Forsworn will come here? I mean, if they attacked Kolskeggr...
Those Forsworn were fierce fighters. Fiercer than I could have imagined.
Thank the Divines we survived that.
Well I'm glad you were there. I don't know how I would have gotten past the Forsworn without you.
That I do. If you want to grab a pickaxe and dig up some iron, feel free. I'll pay for anything you bring me.
Guess we'll have two more mouths to feed for a while.
Don't have much to say unless you're here to mine metal.
Did you hear that? Pavo was driven out of his mine. Almost everyone is dead.
What if the Forsworn come here? We aren't safe.
You should speak with my husband. Skaggi runs everything.
I'm just here to mine.
Got ore to dig.
I'm one of miners. Plenty of ore to dig up around here.
Don't have much to say to you. Got work.
It's amazing how much work goes into the little things, isn't it?
I'm just here to dig. Not much else to talk about.
Thank Malacath we got out of Kolskeggr alive.
Don't know what I would have done without Pavo. Man's braver than he admits.
Another day, another settlement gets attacked. No place in The Reach is safe from the Forsworn.
I'm the hardest worker in Left Hand. Better than any man.
Hope Pavo will be able to go back to his mine soon. Everyone needs a home.
Whatcha doing in Left Hand? Do you dig up rocks, too, like my mom?
Let's just hope that doesn't happen.
Over here! Hurry!
Help! In here!
Backup! I need some help in here!
Intruder! Help!
Now that our "ghost" has been unmasked, I hope we see more visitors in town than just the occasional pilgrim.
Narfi's happy now. Maybe Narfi can sleep. Sleep all night. Sleep, sleep, sleep.
I wish I could make my deliveries more often, but the road's getting dangerous.
I'm going to put those pelts you gave me to good use.
So, have you shown those furry menaces that I mean business?
These damn bears are driving me crazy!
I swear, if one more thing goes wrong, I'm going to burn my mill to the ground!
Miss Temba is much happier now that you took care of those bears. She may not look like she is, but I can tell.
Those bears are making Miss Temba so angry. I hope you can do something about them.
Hello, my friend? What can I do for you on such a fine day?
Such a fine day, smell the air! Isn't it wonderful?
I protest! This is an insult to the dignity of my person! This time I'm completely innocent!
Oh, Ambassador Elenwen, that won't be necessary. It is just Razelan, after all.
I'm sure he'll behave himself for the rest of the evening, won't you dear?
Absolutely not! I protest... uh, that is, yes, of course. I still don't understand what just... oh, never mind.
The head office will hear of this, Razelan! You're a disgrace to the East Empire Company, and to the Empire itself!
Of course! I mean... assuming you don't want me to kill someone for you. I might have to draw the line there.
Just play along.
How dare you! You disgusting pig!
You must be new around here. Bad manners to ask such a direct question, at one of Elenwen's little soirees.
But I have nothing to hide. I'm in from the south, on business.
And if you want to do business in Tamriel these days, well, you'd better get used to cozying up to the Thalmor. Like it or not.
Razelan. And you promised to behave yourself this time.
Ah, pardon me, friend. I didn't see you standing there.
Allow me to introduce myself: Razelan. Imports and exports, by trade. Observer of human nature, by avocation.
Well. Anything to relieve the boredom. As long as it doesn't get back to me.
Remove him. He's disturbing the other guests.
Yes, Madame Ambassador.
Ah, the nectar of the gods! If there's ever anything I can do for you, just name it!
Indeed. Being a drunken boor is thirsty work. And I can't seem to find my way back to the bar...
I find these parties such a dreadful bore.
One thing everyone can agree on. This war is bad for business.
The East Empire Company has interests all across Tamriel, inside and outside the Empire's borders. We try not to get involved in politics.
However, as the head of the company it's my job to make sure we maintain good relationships with prospective partners and investors.
What? I didn't... hmm? No, listen, you must have misunderstood... I don't mean to say I wouldn't be interested... what I mean is...
What does a fellow need to do to get a drink around here?
My friend! A toast to a beautiful friendship!
Mara be praised!
Nightcaller Temple is only a short walk from Dawnstar. Come, we must hurry.
I understand. If you should change your mind, I'll be here.
Turn my back on these people when their lives could be at stake? I should say not.
Those dreams are not just visions, they're a footprint left by the Daedric Lord Vaermina as she drains these people of their memories!
In any other circumstance, I'd agree with you. However, these aren't normal dreams.
The Daedric Lord Vaermina's voracious hunger for memories is responsible for these visions.
The nightmares are an echo of her presence as she feeds.
Who can say? Perhaps she collects them for display like works of art in a nonsensical art gallery.
Whatever the case may be, her intentions are far from benevolent.
Then allow me to introduce myself.
I am Savos Aren, Arch-Mage of the College of Winterhold.
I am quite content to see nearly any aspect of magic explored and investigated here.
But I do not and will not approve of any research or experiments that cause purposeful harm to your fellow members of the College.
Are we clear?
No offense, of course.
You are relatively new here, are you not? I have noticed you, but we have not spoken.
You are new here, are you not? We have not spoken.
No. No I can't.
I have enough to keep me busy. View it as a chance to hone your craft.
New here too, eh? It's good to see another Nord. I feared I'd be the only one.
Why aren't you at your post, Justiciar?
You there. I don't think I've seen you around before.
Haven't seen her around before.
Haven't seen him around before.
Come here. Let me see your face.
Seems a bit short to me.
Wait, is that a tail?
Must be new around here.
The Emperor is going to love it. How can he not?
Gianna will be overjoyed to meet you. She's in the kitchens.
I'm not stealing anything. Besides, some jail time isn't going to make me roll over and play dead.
Well then, if you don't want the extra gold, you can certainly let Letrush keep it as a gesture of goodwill.
Which is utterly useless at the moment. You need to fill it with a soul.
What? No! No, you don't understand! There... there's been some kind of mistake! I... Aggghhh!
Well... if you say so.
That'll teach you to break the law on my watch.
I thought it was finally going to be a quiet day.
You really thought you could get away with it?
Don't get any bright ideas.
Move along. Nothing to see here.
Well, this is a nice mess you've gotten me into.
That could have turned out better.
A few days ago, some thieves from Riften broke in here and stole it! Can you imagine? Took the only thing of value we had.
If you have the guts to head into the Ratway and get my bow back, I'll pay you what I can.
That's it! You found it!
Thanks again for getting my bow back from those thieves. Next time they come knocking I'll be ready.
After we were robbed, Dravin's become more bitter than ever. He hates this place.
No, it can't be... oh my... and all this time, I was thinking he'd been unfaithful to me. I feel so stupid.
So, any luck finding my louse of a husband?
I can't believe my mother's gone. Now what'll I do?
Ah, you're back.
He said he was going east towards the border of Morrowind to trade some of our grain to some folks at Broken Helm Hollow.
I waited and waited, and he never came back. Probably shacked up with some elven whore. Good riddance to him I say.
If I only knew where he was, I'd sure give him a piece of my mind.
Well, hurry it up!
All aboard!
You're the one who's coming to Japhet's Folly with us, right? You ready to go?
I must be on my way now.
Well then, you'd better have something I really want to buy.
Let's just take a look, then.
I hope you've got some coin.
All right, let's not overdo it.
Meet me at my quarters.
Let me give you a bit of advice.
You want to work on something that's not officially allowed? Don't tell anyone you're doing it.
You need something you're not officially supposed to have, you come see me.
You tell anyone that I told you, and I'll fry you from the inside out. Got it? Good.
This is not J'zargo's fault!
By Azura, what's happening?
What's going on?!
This can't be happening!
The Arch-Mage is dead! Dead!
Protect yourself! We're under some kind of attack!
We've got to stay focused.
We never should have brought that thing here!
I hope this isn't something I did.
If this is somehow your fault...
Welcome, people of Solitude! We of the Bards College are pleased to be here to celebrate the Burning of King Olaf.
The festival would not have been possible without the dedication and hard work of our latest applicant.
With the lighting of the effigy...
He becomes a full-fledged member of the Bards College. Please welcome our newest bard!
Good luck. Lucan and I will be waiting for you back in the shop.
Two hundred years ago, I would have lain down my life for the Unholy Matron. But that age has long since passed. Astrid is my matron now.
Without the Night Mother, there is no Dark Brotherhood. The matron deserves our unwavering support and respect.
Though I admit, I find her Keeper a bit too exuberant for my liking.
The witless fool and his pet corpse? Take a guess.
Their arrival is the best thing that's happened to this Sanctuary in years. Astrid is an effective leader, but we've been rudderless for too long.
Scrounging for contracts, abandoning the Tenets. The Dark Brotherhood has been reduced to a group of common cutthroats. Frankly, it's embarrassing.
Truthfully? I'm not entirely sure. It's not that I don't have the utmost respect for Sithis, and the Night Mother, and the ancient ways. I do.
But I've never been much for religion. I kill because it's what I've been trained to do. It's all I know. So long as Astrid is happy, I'm happy.
I don't like mimes, minstrels, thespians, acrobats, jugglers, troubadours, or tumblers. Flutists give me a headache. I particularly hate jesters.
As a rule, I'm also not crazy about the corpses of old women. For the Night Mother, I'll make an exception. But Astrid is the only mistress I serve.
Look. If you're trying to get to know me - don't. I don't like you, and I never will. It's nothing personal. But...
My wife trusts you, and I trust her judgment. So you can't be all bad.
Here's all you need to know: I'm a werewolf. I like killing things. I love Astrid. I hate annoying people. And the color blue gives me a headache.
I might ask the same of you. For you are more than mortal. You are moon-born. You are wolf.
So you're a Companion, then? I can't imagine you got your gift anywhere else. I hope you have better luck with them than I did.
Oh yes, I was once brother to the Companions. Let's just say they found my methods "unsettling." The Dark Brotherhood, obviously, feels differently.
What a curious question. Well, I enjoy moonlit nights, taking long walks on the beach, knitting and unicorns.
In fact, I once took a seaside stroll, on a moonlit night, and discovered a unicorn... which I proceeded to stab in the throat with a crochet needle.
I'm a woman of refined, yet simple tastes.
I'm just a little girl! The Dark Brotherhood killed my mama and papa, and then they took me captive! Please, please help me!
Rather convincing, don't you think?
In truth, I'm no more a little girl than you are. I was once, of course. Three hundred years ago. Vampirism tends to keep one remarkably... fresh.
Hmph. I like to say I was born with a wand in my hand. Well, not literally - you can see how painful that would have been for Mother.
But I was a prodigy! Casting simple spells by one, completing complex incantations by three. Resurrecting corpses by seven! Ha!
When I was thirteen I accidentally burned down the family home. Bit of lightning gone awry. Ah, but soon after I mastered my gifts. Completely.
I went on to teach at the College - left after two years. Too simple. Too safe. None of them truly understood the glory of the Destruction school...
Once, I was a Shadowscale. An assassin in service to the King of Black Marsh. Trained by the Dark Brotherhood since the day of my hatching.
Ah, but that was a lifetime ago. Today, I am the last of my kind. My order is extinct. Now, I happily serve Astrid, and this Sanctuary.
I am a trained killer, and once again have been given purpose. Life is good.
This Sanctuary has been my entire life since I was a young woman. That's when I first discovered my, shall we call it "aptitude," for elimination.
I had an uncle, you see. He made certain... unwanted advances. So I killed him. And liked it. Then I killed again. And liked it even more. And so on.
I was recruited by the Dark Brotherhood, and have been here ever since. I met my husband Arnbjorn, rose to leadership. And now here we are.
Ah, well, I'm Redguard, as I'm sure you've noticed. Though not like most of my kind you'll find in Skyrim, that's for damned sure.
I am a child of Hammerfell, born of the sands of the mighty Alik'r. In that great desert, blood is a shade of crimson I can scarce describe.
My past is long and storied, and not altogether proud. But the Dark Brotherhood saved me from myself. And I have never looked back.
Me? Oh, Cicero is just the Keeper! I... keep! I look after our matron, you see. The Night Mother. I keep her clean, and protected, and happy...
But I am not the Listener. Oh no. There is no Listener. Not yet! But some day, some day, some day I pray, that one will come to hear her say...
The words.
Me? Oh, Cicero is just the Keeper! I... keep! I look after our matron, you see. The Night Mother. I keep her clean, and protected, and happy...
But I am not the Listener. Oh no. You are the Listener! You heard the words! And everything will soon be put right.
Yeah. Follow my wife's orders, and don't do anything stupid.
You must speak to that Muiri woman, correct? I have heard rumors in Windhelm. She was recently implicated in a thievery, along with her lover.
The lover will likely be your target. If memory serves me correctly, he leads a group of bandits. So this may be rather interesting for you.
The contracts I dole out are fairly simple - go to a location, and kill the target. But this is different.
The jobs Astrid gives you will be more important, and usually more complex. Just keep your wits about you. And for Sithis' sake, don't get killed.
You're headed to The Hag's Cure, right? It's a good shop. You should examine their wares. Never pass up the chance to buy a good potion... or poison.
And one more thing. Have fun. You'll be committing a dark deed, but there's no reason you shouldn't enjoy yourself.
You're only young once. Trust me, I know.
Yes. Just because you're now a professional assassin, don't think you have to skulk around in the dark like a skeever and stab people.
Do what I do. Walk up to your target, introduce yourself, melt their skin off, and then run like the wind. Works every time.
You're being sent to meet with a contact directly, is that correct? What a most unusual occurrence. Astrid usually performs this duty herself.
I can only assume she wants to test the full breadth of your abilities. So be polite, and professional, and represent us well.
She said that? If I find anyone working against my wife, or this Sanctuary, I'll beat them to death with their own severed head.
Of course, they'd probably already be dead...
But you know what I mean!
You're joking. While I can understand if some family members wish to honor the Old Ways once more, surely no one would oppose Astrid's rule.
Does she? Listen, brother... Astrid is my mistress, and I worship the ground she walks on. But this Cicero business has her a bit... paranoid.
Does she? Listen, sister... Astrid is my mistress, and I worship the ground she walks on. But this Cicero business has her a bit... paranoid.
Oh! Well, I hope she doesn't suspect me. I mean, I've spoken with Cicero a bit, and he's quite charming. But I'd never go against Astrid. Never.
Ha! And would that surprise anyone? This Sanctuary's a mess. Wouldn't surprise me if somebody's talking to the fool on the sly. Making deals.
And no, before you ask, it's not me. I know which side my bread is buttered on.
To what end? Usurping Astrid's authority as leader of the Sanctuary? Hmmm... Yes, I can see her concern. Thank you for bringing this to my attention.
Oho! The Listener wants my counsel! I thought you were above the opinions of mere mortals, now that you commune with dead old women.
Just remember Astrid's commitment to her Sanctuary, to her Family. A Family that now counts you as one of its own. Honor Astrid above all others.
You may be the Listener, or you may just be a lunatic who's started hearing voices. Either way, Astrid is still your mistress. Not the Night Mother.
The man you need to speak with, his name is Motierre? That's a very old and powerful Breton family, firmly established in Cyrodiil. Most curious...
So you're the Listener, eh? Well, that hardly seems fair. You just got here. The Night Mother should have spoken to me. Any idiot could see that.
But, I will serve gladly, as that's my lot in life. Now let's see. Volunruud. Well, that's an old crypt, infested with draugr. So plan accordingly.
If the Night Mother truly spoke to you, if you really are the Listener... your destiny was written in the Void. Astrid is right to fear your power.
You've got to kill a soft little woman on her wedding day. What's next? Murdering a crippled orphan in his sleep?
Your contract sounds simple, which means it certainly will not be. Getting into that reception will be easy, but have you thought about getting out?
It's no fair. I love weddings. Astrid should have given this contract to me. Instead, she's got me visiting cities, assessing security. How boring.
But listen, I think I can help you. The wedding reception is outside the Temple of the Divines, right? In Solitude?
If so, the bride will probably address the crowd from the balcony. It overlooks the courtyard.
Well, there's an old statue there. I've seen it. Hanging right over where she'll be standing. Old means weak. Weak means it could fall. Hmmm?
Ah, killing her will be easy. She'll be so happy, she'll never see it coming. I should know, I killed a bride once.
It was our honeymoon. Tepid, I said! The bathwater should be tepid! Lukewarm is not tepid! Close, maybe, but there is a difference! Stupid cow.
I hope you don't mind, but I took the liberty of surveying the site of the reception.
There's a small parapet, just opposite the balcony, that would prove an excellent spot for a long-range kill.
I left something there for you. If you're inclined to take that approach.
What's the matter, Listener? Night Mother not feeling very chatty these days? You need help from your flesh and blood lessers?
You've got to track a man, kill him, and then frame him for a crime. This contract's perfect... for me. Clearly, my lovely wife made a mistake.
Astrid had me investigate the Penitus Oculatus. Their members are highly trained, effective warriors. Be on your guard, brother.
Astrid had me investigate the Penitus Oculatus. Their members are highly trained, effective warriors. Be on your guard, sister.
Killing Gaius Maro in a city will be challenging because of all the guards, but fights, even murder, are fairly common.
Killing him secretly will certainly make things easier on you, but if there are witnesses, so be it.
Normally I talk to a victim before I end his life, but I wouldn't recommend it this time. Maro will be alert to danger. Talking may spook him.
Damn dustmen! Why don't you stay dead?
My place is chief and my challenge is to lead my people.
Do not allow Ataxia to fester. A smart warrior keeps a potion to cure diseases at all times.
Stormcloaks!
Just what I was afraid of!
Damn dustmen!
Why don't you stay dead?
If you wish to trade, see me during daylight.
Shuftharz can forge you some armor, outlander.
How about an axe? Malacath smiles on the axe-wielder.
The forge is strong and so is our chief, Larak. I'm content.
Then I suggest you "collect" it. Until you pay the fine, you are effectively cut off from all Dark Brotherhood duties and contracts.
Good. I'll spread the word that you've made reparations. Now let's forget the past and get back to business...
Excellent. Now we can get back to business. I think it's best we just forget this ever happened and move on, hmm?
Not ready? Well, my arrogant little sibling, until you pay the fine the Dark Brotherhood holds nothing for you. No contracts. No rewards. No respect.
I understand, Listener. And I hope you understand that until the fine is paid, I must insist you refrain from all Dark Brotherhood business.
You disrespected the Brotherhood. And it'll cost you. You want back into the Family, you pay the price. 500 gold, and all is forgiven.
You may be Listener, and head of the Sanctuary, but your recent behavior sets a bad example. I must insist you pay a fine of 300 gold.
If the Tenets were still in place, you would be required to defeat the Wrath of Sithis. Now you just need to pay a fine to Nazir. Ah, the Old Ways...
When you disrespect your Family, you disrespect yourself. Don't you care for anything at all? Prove that you do - speak with Nazir, make reparations.
Bah. If it were up to me, we'd cut off your hands and never let you back in this Sanctuary. But no. All you have to do is pay Nazir a fine. Pathetic.
Ah, don't worry, we all like to break the rules sometimes. Just see Nazir and pay the fine. Seems I do that at least once a month...
There are so few rules here. All we ask is that you don't disrespect your Family. But for some reason, you have. Best talk to Nazir and pay the fine.
Idiot. I may not have a lot of rules, but that doesn't give you free rein to do as you please. You want to resume your duties? Go talk to Nazir.
I'm not a coward, outlander. You want me to prove it? Fine. My axe is yours. To the most dangerous corners of Skyrim and back.
That you have. Here.
Fair fight. Here you are.
All right. I'll lay off him. Just leave.
It's got to be the Imperials! Find them!
Look out! Draugr!
Look out! Draugr!
Well, aren't you sweet? I'd be happy to give you some alchemy training in exchange for the ring, if you bring it to me.
It should be careful with those flames.
You're not supposed to play with fire, you know.
Take care not to set anything on fire.
You're not afraid you'll set fire to something?
What kind of fool plays with fire like that?
You're not planning on burning anything with that, are you?
Careful not to torch anything important.
I do hope you know what you're doing with those flames...
Try not to set anything on fire, all right?
Fire is not a thing to be played with...
Careful with that fire.
What's the matter with you? You want to start a blaze?
Dear, you really ought to be careful with fire.
Better not set anything on fire.
What do you think you're doing? Knock it off with the flames already.
Is it getting hot, or is it just me?
Whoa. Hope you know what you're doing with those flames there.
It is planning on starting a fire?
Hey now, you be careful with that fire.
I wish I could play with fire like that.
Take care with those flames.
Mind yourself with that fire. Don't burn anything down, now.
Have a fascination with fire, do we?
Umm... don't suppose you'd want to take that fire somewhere else? You know... somewhere away from me?
Careful with those flames, unless you plan on torching something.
Fire!
Don't set anything on fire, all right?
Fire is not a toy.
Don't go setting the place on fire, okay?
Plan on burning everything down, do you? Damn fool.
You should be more careful with those flames.
Those flames get out of control, and you're gonna wish they hadn't.
Careful, now. Wouldn't want someone to get... burned.
Careful with that fire.
You've got that fire under control. Right?
Really? I dunno... I don't feel invisible.
You're dumb.
Come on. Hurry it up already. The waiting is killing me.
Hey... So are you ever going to make me invisible or what?
Really? I mean... I still feel like I'm here.
All right! I knew you'd do it. This is going to be so fun. You can't even imagine!
Ooh, you're a wizard! I mean, you cast that spell just now, yeah? I was hoping you could make me invisible or something. Can you? Make me invisible?
I can't wait to be invisible.
Awww. And here I thought you could do it...
Well, I'll just have to think of something else, then. Bye.
Boo! Ahahahaha!
Oooooh... weeeeoooooo...
That's right. Who's invisible? Me!
Surprise! Ahaha!
Okay, great!
Now I'm finally going to have some fun around here.
Bye!
I can't believe it's come to this.
